text:
INPATIENT ONCOLOGY PROGRESS NOTE
Hospital Day #5
Service: Medical Oncology

**PATIENT:** Diane Castellano, 55F
**MRN:** [on file]
**ATTENDING:** [Oncology Attending]
**ADMITTING DX:** Immune-mediated colitis, grade 3, in setting of ipilimumab/nivolumab (Yervoy/Opdivo) therapy

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR HOSPITALIZATION:
Ms. Castellano is a 55-year-old female landscape architect with stage IV BRAF V600E-mutated melanoma, previously treated with dabrafenib/trametinib (Tafinlar/Mekinist) then transitioned to combination ipilimumab + nivolumab (Yervoy/Opdivo) following hepatic and systemic progression with new brain metastases at 8 months. She underwent SRS to 2 brain lesions prior to starting ipi/nivo. She is now admitted on hospital day 5 for grade 3 immune-mediated colitis after cycle 2 of ipi/nivo. She initially presented to the ED with 10-12 watery, non-bloody stools per day over 4 days with associated crampy lower abdominal pain, low-grade fever to 38.1°C, and significant fatigue. She was admitted and started on IV methylprednisolone.

---

INTERVAL HISTORY (HD#5 update):
Overall, Ms. Castellano is improving. After initiation of IV methylprednisolone 2 mg/kg/day (solumedrol), stool frequency has decreased from 10-12 episodes/day at admission to 3-4 loose stools today. Abdominal cramping is much improved. She reports her energy is slightly better. No fever today. No hematochezia. No nausea or vomiting. She is tolerating a low-residue oral diet as of yesterday. She had a small amount of oral intake yesterday and her appetite is slowly returning.

GI / Sigmoidoscopy results (reviewed, performed HD#2 by gastroenterology): diffuse colonic mucosal erythema and friability involving the sigmoid and descending colon consistent with immune-mediated colitis; no evidence of CMV inclusions on pathology (CMV immunostain negative); stool cultures from admission: no pathogen isolated; C. difficile toxin PCR: negative; stool ova and parasites: pending.

No infectious etiology identified. Presentation and endoscopic appearance consistent with immune checkpoint inhibitor-associated colitis.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS (interval, per nursing assessment and patient report this morning):
- GI: diarrhea improving (3-4 stools today vs. 10-12 at admit); cramping improved; no blood per rectum; nausea resolved; tolerating POs
- Constitutional: fatigue present but improved; no fever today (Tmax 37.4 overnight)
- Skin: mild maculopapular rash on chest/upper back (noted after cycle 1, grade 1, continues; not worsening)
- Neuro: no new headache; no visual changes; no focal deficits (followed post-SRS)
- Pulm: no cough, no dyspnea
- MSK: no arthralgias
- Endocrine: no symptoms of adrenal insufficiency (cortisol AM level checked HD#3 — within normal limits per notes)

---

PAST ONCOLOGIC HISTORY:
- Stage IV melanoma, BRAF V600E+, initially presenting with hepatic, lymph node, and subcutaneous metastases; brain MRI negative at diagnosis
- NGS (FoundationOne or equivalent): BRAF V600E, TMB 18.4 mut/Mb
- Line 1: dabrafenib + trametinib — initial 4-week assessment showed early response; 3-month scan with marked response; 6-month scan with stabilization; 8-month imaging showed systemic progression and new brain metastases
- SRS delivered to 2 brain lesions (details per radiation oncology note)
- Line 2: ipilimumab 3 mg/kg + nivolumab 1 mg/kg q3w started per standard melanoma dosing
  - After cycle 1: mild fatigue, grade 1 maculopapular rash, managed conservatively
  - After cycle 2: grade 3 diarrhea/colitis as above — current admission

PMH (non-oncologic):
- No prior autoimmune disease
- No prior IBD
- Hypertension (managed outpatient; held antihypertensives during acute illness per primary team)

Social History: landscape architect, southwestern US; non-smoker; social EtOH; married with adult children; strong family support

Allergies: NKDA

Home medications (pre-admission):
- Amlodipine 5 mg daily (held)
- Lisinopril 10 mg daily (held)
- Loratadine PRN (seasonal allergies)

Current inpatient medications:
- IV methylprednisolone 2 mg/kg/day (continuing, now day 5)
- IV fluids (LR, currently at KVO)
- Pantoprazole 40 mg IV daily
- Loperamide PRN (used sparingly given colitis; avoiding in setting of active diarrhea)
- VTE prophylaxis: enoxaparin (SQ) dosed per pharmacy

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:

Vitals (0800 today):
- Temp: 37.2°C | BP: 118/72 | HR: 84 bpm | RR: 16 | SpO2: 98% on RA
- Weight: 62.1 kg (admission weight 61.8 kg — near baseline, mild fluid balance improvement)

Intake/Output (last 24h):
- I: ~1,850 mL (oral + IV)
- O: ~2,100 mL (urine + stool estimated)
- Net: approximately -250 mL; improved from prior days of more significant net negative

General: Alert, oriented x3. Fatigued-appearing but more interactive than prior days. In no acute distress.

HEENT: Mucous membranes moist. Oropharynx clear. No oral ulcerations.

Skin: Grade 1 maculopapular rash on anterior chest and upper back, unchanged from prior assessment; no desquamation, no blistering, not pruritic currently.

Cardiovascular: RRR, no murmurs. No peripheral edema.

Respiratory: CTA bilaterally, no wheezes or crackles.

Abdomen: Soft. Mildly tender to deep palpation in left lower quadrant. No rebound, no guarding. Bowel sounds present. No distension. No organomegaly appreciated.

Extremities: No edema. Peripheral pulses intact.

Neurological: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. No focal deficits. Gait not formally assessed today (bed rest).

---

PERTINENT LAB RESULTS (today, and trend):

CBC:
- WBC 11.2 (mild leukocytosis, likely steroid effect; was 14.1 on admission)
- Hgb 11.4 (stable)
- Plt 218

CMP:
- Na 138, K 3.7, Cl 101, CO2 24
- BUN 10, Cr 0.72 (improved from 0.92 on admission; previously mildly elevated in setting of dehydration)
- Glucose 178 (elevated; steroid-induced hyperglycemia — sliding scale insulin in place, monitoring)
- LFTs: ALT 28, AST 22, ALP 74, T.bili 0.6 (within normal limits; reassuring)

Inflammatory markers:
- CRP 18 mg/L (down from 68 mg/L on admission)
- ESR 42 (trending down)

Thyroid (checked HD#3): TSH 1.8 (normal)
AM Cortisol (HD#3): 14.2 mcg/dL (adequate; no evidence of adrenal insufficiency)

Stool studies (HD#1/2):
- C. diff PCR: negative
- Stool cultures: no pathogen
- CMV colitis (tissue immunostain HD#2): negative
- O&P: pending

---

IMAGING / PROCEDURES (this admission):
- Flex sigmoidoscopy (HD#2, GI): diffuse erythema and friability sigmoid/descending colon; biopsies taken. Path: active colitis with increased intraepithelial lymphocytes, no granulomas, no CMV inclusions. Pattern consistent with checkpoint inhibitor colitis.
- CT abdomen/pelvis (HD#1, ordered to r/o perforation or toxic megacolon): diffuse colonic wall thickening, greatest in sigmoid and descending colon; no free air; no pneumatosis; no abscess. Findings compatible with colitis.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

Ms. Castellano is a 55-year-old woman with stage IV BRAF V600E-mutated melanoma on ipi/nivo (Yervoy/Opdivo) presenting with grade 3 immune-mediated colitis after cycle 2. She is now on hospital day 5 of IV methylprednisolone and is demonstrating meaningful clinical improvement — stool frequency reduced to 3-4 episodes/day (from >10 at admission), abdominal pain markedly improved, tolerating oral diet, afebrile, and inflammatory markers trending down.

1. Immune-mediated colitis, grade 3:
   - Continue IV methylprednisolone 2 mg/kg/day through today (HD#5)
   - Given sustained improvement to approximately grade 1 severity (3-4 loose stools/day), plan to transition to oral prednisone 1 mg/kg/day (approximately 60 mg daily given weight ~62 kg) starting tomorrow (HD#6)
   - If continues to improve on oral steroids over next 24-48 hours, patient will be appropriate for discharge
   - Gastroenterology co-management ongoing; appreciate their involvement and sigmoidoscopy — no further endoscopic intervention planned at this time
   - Infliximab (Remicade) was considered but not initiated given adequate and clear response to corticosteroids; held in reserve if colitis recurs or worsens after steroid taper
   - At discharge: oral prednisone taper over approximately 8 weeks (reduce by ~10 mg every 1-2 weeks, guided by symptoms); written taper instructions will be provided
   - GI follow-up scheduled at 2-4 weeks post-discharge
   - Stool diary to be kept at home

2. Immunotherapy management:
   - Ipilimumab (Yervoy) PERMANENTLY DISCONTINUED per ASCO/SITC guidelines for grade 3 immune-mediated colitis — patient and family counseled, in agreement
   - Nivolumab (Opdivo) held; may consider cautious resumption after (a) complete resolution of colitis symptoms to grade 0-1, AND (b) successful steroid taper to <10 mg/day prednisone equivalent — anticipated approximately 8-10 weeks from now
   - Benefits vs. risks of resuming nivolumab alone discussed at length with patient; she understands there is a risk of colitis recurrence but expressed understanding and desire to continue cancer treatment when medically appropriate
   - Will monitor closely with nivolumab rechallenge if pursued

3. Steroid-induced hyperglycemia:
   - Blood glucose 178 today; on sliding scale insulin (Humalog)
   - Continue monitoring fingerstick glucose QID while inpatient
   - At discharge: patient education re: home glucose monitoring; if values persistently >200, will initiate outpatient management — will coordinate with PCP

4. Melanoma disease status:
   - Most recent systemic imaging (approximately 3 months prior) with mixed but early response to ipi/nivo after cycle 1
   - No new neurologic symptoms; will not pursue urgent re-imaging during this admission unless clinical change — brain MRI and restaging CT to be planned as outpatient once steroid taper underway

5. Skin rash (grade 1, maculopapular):
   - Unchanged; not requiring systemic treatment at this time
   - Continue monitoring; topical triamcinolone available PRN if pruritus develops

6. VTE prophylaxis:
   - Continue enoxaparin while inpatient

7. Disposition:
   - Anticipate discharge home tomorrow (HD#6) if tolerating oral diet and stool frequency remains ≤4/day on oral steroids
   - Discharge with: oral prednisone + taper schedule, pantoprazole, glucose monitoring instructions, oncology and GI follow-up
   - Patient lives with husband; strong support at home; able to manage oral medications
   - Patient instructed to call or present to ED for any recurrence of high stool frequency, blood per rectum, fever, or abdominal pain

---

COMMUNICATION:
Discussed plan with patient and her husband at bedside this morning. Questions answered regarding colitis management, steroid taper, and rationale for permanent ipilimumab discontinuation. Patient expressed relief that diarrhea is improving. Emotional support provided regarding treatment change; reassured that nivolumab alone remains an active treatment option once fully recovered.

Gastroenterology aware of plan; no additional recommendations at this time.

Primary team updated.

---

Time spent: approximately 35 minutes (>50% counseling and care coordination)

[Electronically signed — Attending, Medical Oncology]
